title: WebWatcher: A Multimodal Agent for Deep Research

defines.name: WebWatcher
defines.definition: A multimodal deep research web agent that combines visual-language reasoning with multiple external tools, synthetic training trajectories, and reinforcement learning.

defines.name: BrowseComp-VL
defines.definition: A BrowseComp-style benchmark for complex visual-textual information retrieval and reasoning.

problem: Existing deep research agents are mostly text-centric and rigidly tool-driven, making them unable to robustly solve complex multimodal information-seeking tasks that require integrated visual and textual reasoning.
